title: Open-Meteo Forecast Series (Seamless Time Series)
description: STORAGE SCHEMA for seamless historical forecast time series from Open-Meteo. One row per (station, model, valid_at). These are NOT discrete model runs — each value comes from whichever model cycle was most recent, assembled into a continuous series. No issued_at provenance. Supplementary source for richer variables.

model.description: Open-Meteo model name. NAM excluded (archive starts Sep 2025, not yet available).

valid_at.description: When this forecast value is for (UTC, Z-suffix). Whole-hour aligned.

ingested_at.description: When we fetched this record (UTC, Z-suffix). Null for historical backfill.

temperature_c.description: Air temperature at 2m in Celsius. Open-Meteo 'temperature_2m'. Float64, no rounding.

wind_speed_kmh.description: Wind speed at 10m in km/h. Open-Meteo 'wind_speed_10m'.

wind_direction_deg.description: Wind direction at 10m in degrees (0-360). Open-Meteo 'wind_direction_10m'.

wind_gusts_kmh.description: Wind gusts at 10m in km/h. Open-Meteo 'wind_gusts_10m'.

pressure_msl_hpa.description: Mean sea level pressure in hPa. Open-Meteo 'pressure_msl'.

precipitation_mm.description: Total precipitation in mm. Open-Meteo 'precipitation'. Zero = real zero, not missing.

shortwave_radiation_wm2.description: Global horizontal shortwave radiation in W/m2. Open-Meteo 'shortwave_radiation'. NWP models may return small negative values (~-3 W/m2) at night due to floating-point post-processing noise — stored as-reported per RAW-AS-REPORTED rule.

direct_radiation_wm2.description: Direct beam radiation on horizontal surface in W/m2. Open-Meteo 'direct_radiation'. NWP models may return small negative values at night due to floating-point post-processing noise — stored as-reported.

cape_jkg.description: Convective Available Potential Energy in J/kg. Open-Meteo 'cape'.

snow_depth_m.description: Snow depth on ground in meters. Open-Meteo 'snow_depth'.

$comment: ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS: (1) SEAMLESS TIME SERIES, not discrete model runs. No issued_at — each value comes from whichever model cycle was most recent. Open-Meteo assembles this from initial hours of successive model runs. (2) Cannot answer 'what did 12Z GFS predict?' — only 'what was the best available short-range forecast?' This is why IEM (discrete runs) is primary. (3) Null = model horizon exceeded or data unavailable. Zero precip = real zero (confirmed via API testing). Never conflated. (4) Native Open-Meteo metric units: °C, hPa, km/h, mm. No conversions needed. (5) valid_at must be whole-hour aligned, UTC with Z suffix. (6) Dedup: exact-duplicate on (station_code, model, valid_at), first-seen wins. (7) Per-model archive start dates enforced at ingest: gfs_seamless=2021-03-23, ncep_hrrr_conus=2018-01-01, ncep_nbm_conus=2024-10-08, ecmwf_ifs025=2024-02-03, ecmwf_aifs025=2025-02-20. NAM excluded (archive starts Sep 2025). (8) 21 hourly variables selected from 80+ available. Additional variables (pressure-level, soil, 15-min) deferred.
